She was subsequently, aggressively sold to the English, leading to her massive, incredibly infamous trial for heresy and her horrific, absolute execution by burning at the stake a year later.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)

On May 23, 1984, Bachendri Pal achieved a massive, incredibly historic, and deeply awe-inspiring mountaineering milestone. Battling incredibly aggressive weather and extreme altitude, she successfully and heavily summited the absolute peak of Mount Everest. She became the absolute, very first Indian woman—and only the fifth woman globally—to aggressively conquer the world's massive, highest mountain. Her monumental achievement heavily inspired a massive generation of Indian women to aggressively pursue extreme sports and mountaineering.

On May 23, 1891, P. B. Gajendragadkar, a massive, highly distinguished Indian jurist, was born in Maharashtra. He achieved massive, towering legal prominence by serving as the 7th Chief Justice of India. His massive, highly progressive judicial tenure heavily championed massive labor reforms and aggressively defended the fundamental rights enshrined in the Constitution, deeply shaping the massive, foundational jurisprudence of the early, independent Indian republic.
